name: Shipping / Tanker Rates
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **TANKER PARADOX: Frontline +2.8% to $38.10 / BOAT +3.5% to $42.97 RALLY DESPITE BRENT COLLAPSE -1.7% to $107.96.** This is the cleanest signal of MARKET BIFURCATION today — kinetic-war probability priced down (oil down, defense down, VIX down), but physical chokepoint persists (tankers up). Trump's May 5 pause was on the US convoy operation, NOT on Iran's closure of Hormuz — blockade 'remains in full force' per Trump's own Truth Social framing. VLCC TD3C MEG-China sustained ~$420-474K/day per Lloyd's List/Baltic Exchange — historically extreme; up from Wk4 $112K and Wk11 $326K levels. Hormuz transits restricted; vessels prefer non-Iran-nexus operators (Frontline benefits). The market is pricing two separate things: (1) US-Iran kinetic war probability is lower (oil/defense/VIX down), (2) Hormuz physical chokepoint persists (tanker rates high, AWRP elevated). For deployment thesis: 'long tankers / short crude' remains the dispersion trade. Reversibility: if Pakistan-mediated track delivers Iran formal Hormuz reopening within 14-21 days (by May 26), tanker stocks reprice -15-25% (rates collapse to normalized levels). Until then, persistent. AWRP ~1% Hull&Mach value/7-day per Argus Media — pause likely cuts further toward 0.5% normalization threshold over May 6-12 if Pakistan track concretely advances.
